description_long: This is a weathered stone grave marker featuring a prominent Celtic cross at the top, characterized by its circular ring connecting the cross arms. The surface is mottled with patches of lichen and discoloration, indicating significant age and exposure to the elements. The base is rectangular and sits on a patch of grass, with some moss and earth visible around the foundation. The cross itself is thick and solid, with four circular cutouts at the intersection of the arms and the ring. The stone appears rough and pitted, with no clearly legible inscriptions visible from any angle. The overall form is monolithic and sturdy, typical of traditional grave markers found in older cemeteries.
description: A weathered stone grave marker with a Celtic cross, mottled with lichen and set on a grassy base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the Celtic cross and the rectangular base, with visible lichen spots.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the cross and the depth of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view of the grave marker, showing the back of the cross and more discoloration on the stone.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness of the cross and the rectangular base.
